title: Aligner ESLint majors (BE v10 vs FE/Web v9) — path (b) BE descends to v9.39.4

niceToHave.title: TD-16 hunk piggybacks T1.4 worktree (from T1.3 spec)
niceToHave.why: The T1.4 diff /tmp/audit-s1-T1.4-green.diff contains 2 distinct hunks on docs/TECH_DEBT.md: (1) TD-7 statut+jsdoc correction (in-scope T1.4), (2) TD-16 new entry for noUncheckedIndexedAccess (was T1.3's deliverable, already reviewed/approved as T1.3-review-loop1.json). Not a defect because T1.3 ALSO ships only TD-16 hunk per its own green-diff (see T1.3 review § verifications.scopeBoundary noting MASTER.md was concurrent agent), and the worktree at HEAD already had T1.3's TECH_DEBT.md staged. T1.4 added on top. Slight non-isolation; orchestrator should ensure final commit messages distinguish authorship (T1.3 owns TD-16, T1.4 owns TD-7 edits).
niceToHave.fix: No action required at review time. At commit-split time, ensure two commits or one commit message references both T1.3 (TD-16) and T1.4 (TD-7+package.json+lockfile).

niceToHave.title: museum-frontend/package-lock.json hunk = T1.5 contamination (not T1.4)
niceToHave.why: git diff --stat HEAD museum-frontend/ shows a 1-line edit (`musaium-shared 0.1.0 → 0.2.0`). This is sibling task T1.5 (musaium-shared cull/bump), NOT a T1.4 scope violation — T1.4 only touches museum-backend ESLint deps. Verified the change does not relate to ESLint. Informational only.
niceToHave.fix: No action for T1.4 reviewer. Orchestrator must ensure FE package-lock.json hunk lands with T1.5's commit, not T1.4's.

verifications.scopeBoundary: PASS — Within T1.4's declared scope (museum-backend ESLint + TECH_DEBT.md TD-7), the green-diff /tmp/audit-s1-T1.4-green.diff matches expectations: museum-backend/package.json (2 lines: `eslint ^10.2.0 → ^9.39.4`, `@eslint/js ^10.0.1 → ^9.39.4`), museum-backend/pnpm-lock.yaml (regen, +200/-134, expected for ESLint major downgrade with transitive shifts), docs/TECH_DEBT.md TD-7 hunk (statut update + jsdoc 62.7 claim correction). Worktree additionally shows TD-16 hunk (T1.3 owner, already approved separately) and museum-frontend/package-lock.json single-line bump (T1.5 owner). Both are sibling-task artifacts, not T1.4 scope violations. find docs/adr -name 'ADR-053*' = empty (spec Q3 honored: no ADR-053 created, TECH_DEBT update preferred).
verifications.antiHallucination: PASS — Verified: (1) `grep -n '"eslint":\|"@eslint/js":' museum-backend/package.json` returns lines 79 (`@eslint/js: ^9.39.4`) and 100 (`eslint: ^9.39.4`), matching spec target verbatim. (2) museum-backend/node_modules/eslint/package.json `"version": "9.39.4"` confirms install resolved to v9 maintenance track. (3) Read docs/TECH_DEBT.md:194 — original phrase `forcerait aussi eslint-plugin-jsdoc < 62.7` is REPLACED by `vérifié 2026-05-16 — eslint-plugin-jsdoc@62.9 peer = ^7..^10, aucune cascade nécessaire`. Old wording not present in current file. (4) grep -n '^### TD-7' returns line 166, surrounding context confirms statut bullet (line 168) now reads `partiellement clos — BE rebaselined v9.39.4 (2026-05-16, T1.4)`. (5) lockfile head (`lockfileVersion: '9.0'`) + 246 occurrences of `eslint` confirm regen reflects ESLint dep tree.
verifications.regressionGuards: PASS — Re-ran sanity gates with downgraded ESLint: (1) `cd museum-backend && pnpm lint` exit 0 — runs eslint src/ (--max-warnings=0) + pnpm lint:test-discipline + tsc --noEmit. No errors, no warnings. ESLint v9 honors all current rules from `typescript-eslint@8.58.1`, `eslint-plugin-import-x@4.16.2`, `eslint-plugin-boundaries@6.0.1`, `eslint-plugin-jsdoc@62.9.0`, `eslint-plugin-unicorn@64.0.0`, `eslint-plugin-sonarjs@4.0.3`, `eslint-plugin-n@17.24.0`, `eslint-plugin-check-file@3.3.1`, `eslint-plugin-musaium-test-discipline@0.1.0` (peer >=8 <11, v9 satisfies). (2) `node scripts/sentinels/as-any-ratchet.mjs` = `PASS — backend=0<=0 frontend=0<=0 web=0<=0`. No regression on the as-any baseline. (3) No FE/Web package.json touched (`git diff HEAD museum-frontend/package.json museum-web/package.json` empty), so FE/Web ESLint untouched, no risk of cross-app regression.
verifications.specCompliance:
- AC path (b) BE descends to v9: MET (package.json:79+100 both `^9.39.4`, exactly spec target).
- AC `pnpm bootstrap` clean: MET indirectly (lockfile regen +200/-134 with no resolution conflicts; pnpm lint runs install-clean afterwards).
- AC décision documentée dans CHANGELOG ou ADR: MET via TECH_DEBT.md TD-7 update (spec § Open Questions Q3 explicitly retains TECH_DEBT route, rejects ADR-053).
- Spec § Tasks T1.4.b (2 lines edit): MET verbatim.
- Spec § Tasks T1.4.g (TD-7 statut update, NO ADR-053): MET — statut now `partiellement clos — BE rebaselined v9.39.4 (2026-05-16, T1.4)`, AND `find docs/adr -name ADR-053*` empty.
- Spec § Open Questions Q2 (correct TD-7:194 false claim about jsdoc < 62.7): MET — phrase replaced with verified-2026-05-16 claim that eslint-plugin-jsdoc@62.9 peer is ^7..^10. Direct UFR-013 honesty improvement, the prior text was factually wrong.
- Spec ratchet `lintWarningsBackend` (T1.4.f): NOT regressed — pnpm lint exit 0 means 0 warnings (--max-warnings=0 enforces), well under the 50 ceiling; no update strictly required.
verifications.honestyAuditUFR013: PASS — The TD-7 alternative `Downgrade BE → v9` previously contained an unverified claim (`forcerait aussi eslint-plugin-jsdoc < 62.7`). T1.4 spec § Open Questions Q2 explicitly identified this as factually false (verified 4 versions 62.5→62.9, all peer `^7..^10`), and the diff replaces it with the corrected, dated claim. This is exemplary UFR-013 behavior: not just shipping the downgrade, but auditing and correcting prior documentation that justified deferring the downgrade. The new wording is auditable (cites npm view peerDependencies semantics) and dated.

summary: T1.4 cleanly executes spec path (b): museum-backend ESLint downgrades to v9.39.4 (line 79 + line 100, exactly matching FE/Web), lockfile regens consistently, and TD-7 is updated to `partiellement clos` with the BE rebaseline credited to T1.4. The diff also opportunistically corrects a factually wrong claim in the TD-7 rejected-alternatives section (jsdoc < 62.7 cascade), which is a direct UFR-013 win. Sanity gates remain green: `pnpm lint` exit 0 under ESLint v9, as-any ratchet PASS. No ADR-053 created (spec Q3 honored). Two informational nice-to-haves flag that the worktree carries piggyback hunks from T1.3 (TD-16) and T1.5 (FE package-lock musaium-shared bump) which are sibling-task artifacts, not T1.4 scope violations — orchestrator should split commits by owner. Approved with weighted mean 96.4.
